# Prescriber Guide

## Who this guide is for

External partners — accounting firms, law firms, IT integrators — who review and co-sign AI compliance documents for their clients on Trustixy.

---

## 1. Understanding Your Role

As a prescriber partner, you have two functions:

**Distribution** — you invite your clients to Trustixy and help them get their SDK installed and systems classified.

**Co-signature** — you review AI system classifications and compliance documents generated by Trustixy, then professionally endorse them. This transforms an AI-generated document into a professionally reviewed deliverable.

### What co-signature means (and what it doesn't)

By co-signing, you confirm that you have:
1. Reviewed the AI system description and use case
2. Verified the classification is appropriate given your professional judgment
3. Validated that the listed obligations are applicable and complete
4. Applied your expertise to the overall compliance assessment

Co-signature is a professional review — not a legal guarantee of full regulatory compliance. The client remains responsible for implementing the obligations. The scope is defined in your partner agreement.

---

## 2. Becoming a Partner

1. Go to trustixy.com and click **Become a partner**
2. Fill in the application form: organization name, type, contact, number of SME clients
3. Choose your model:

**Per-act co-signature** *(accounting firms, consultants)*
No subscription. You set your own co-signature fee (€150–400 recommended). Trustixy takes €25 per act; you collect the rest via Stripe Connect.

**Integrator license** *(IT service companies, ESN/SSII)*
€399/month, unlimited usage and API access. The compliance report becomes a standard project deliverable — billed to your client at your own price.

| Model | Trustixy cost | Your revenue |
|---|---|---|
| **Per act** | €25/co-signature | Your fee × volume |
| **Integrator** | €399/month | Project billing margin |

Submit the form — you'll receive a response within 48 hours. On approval: partner agreement to sign + access to the Partner Portal.

---

## 3. Scope Acceptance (at Signup)

**Scope acceptance is part of the partner application form.** No separate page or flow.

During your application, you will read the **Co-signature Scope Document** and check an acceptance box. On approval, co-signature is immediately unlocked for all risk levels.

The scope document defines exactly what your co-signature confirms and what it does not. Read it carefully before submitting your application.

**Before applying**, we recommend:
- Checking with your professional indemnity insurer that co-signing AI-generated compliance documents falls within your covered practice
- Reviewing the position of your professional association on AI-assisted compliance deliverables

---

## 4. Configuring Billing (Per-act Model)

1. In the Partner Portal, go to **Settings → Billing**
2. Click **Connect Stripe account** and complete the Stripe Connect onboarding (~5 minutes)
3. Set your **co-signature fee** — displayed to your client when they submit a request
4. Trustixy deducts €25 per transaction; you receive the remainder automatically

View transaction history under **Billing → Transactions**.

---

## 5. Inviting Clients

1. Partner Portal → **Clients → Invite client**
2. Enter client organization name and contact email
3. Click **Send invitation**

The client receives an email with a link to create their Trustixy account. Their account is automatically linked to your partner profile — they never need to search for you.

**Track onboarding status:**
- Invited · Active · In progress · Compliant · Needs review

---

## 6. Co-signing Compliance Documents

### Where to find requests

The Partner Portal shows your **Signature Queue** on the home page. You also receive an email notification for each new request.

### Reviewing a document

Click a signature request to see:
- System name, description, use case, data used, autonomy level
- Risk level, explanation, confidence score
- Obligations list with regulatory references
- Full document preview
- SDK evidence (if the client has the SDK installed): total sessions, action types, affected resources

Review with your professional judgment:
- Is the system description accurate?
- Is the risk level appropriate?
- Are all obligations listed and applicable?

### Adding professional notes (recommended)

Add a **Reviewer Comments** section before deciding. Recommended for high-risk systems or cases with gaps. Your notes appear as a distinct section in the document and PDF.

Examples:
- "Classification assumes internal use only. If deployed for external clients, re-classification required."
- "Risk level confirmed as Limited. Client should verify their AI provider's own EU AI Act status."

### Your decision

**Approve & Sign:** Your name, firm, and date are embedded in the document. The client is notified immediately.

**Request Changes:** Enter your notes. The client updates and re-submits — a new request appears in your queue.

**Decline:** Use only if you cannot review (conflict of interest, outside your expertise). The client is advised to find another reviewer.

Once co-signed, your name and firm are permanently embedded in the document and audit trail. A new document version must be generated for any future updates.

---

## 7. Monitoring Your Client Portfolio

Partner Portal home page shows:
- Total active clients
- Compliance health breakdown (compliant / needs review / at risk)
- Pending signature requests

Click any client to see all their registered systems, classification dates, risk levels, and document status.

**Export a client compliance report:** From the client detail view, click **Export compliance report** to download a PDF covering all their systems, statuses, and document trail.

---

## 8. Managing Projects (Integrator Model)

### Create a project
1. Partner Portal → **Projects → New project**
2. Enter project name and link to a client organization

### Generate a compliance report
Once the client has classified their AI systems:
1. Open the project → click **Generate compliance report**
2. Download the PDF and include it in your project deliverables

### API access
Your integrator license includes API access to automate report generation. See `specs/api.md` for the `/integrator/projects` endpoints.

---

## 9. Marketing Assets

Download from **Settings → Marketing assets**:
- Partner pitch deck (editable)
- Client one-pager
- Email templates for client outreach
- Partner badge (web + print)

**Recommended positioning:** "AI compliance advisory, delivered" — you advise, Trustixy handles the registry and documentation, you co-sign to make it professionally valid. Natural extension of your existing compliance practice.

---

## 10. Frequently Asked Questions

**What if I disagree with the AI classification?**
Request changes and explain why. Your professional judgment always takes precedence.

**Am I legally liable for systems I co-sign?**
Co-signature confirms a professional review within the scope defined in your partner agreement — not a compliance guarantee. Review the scope with your own legal counsel before your first co-signature.

**Can I co-sign for clients not linked to my account?**
No. Co-signature is only available for clients in your Trustixy partner account.

**Can multiple people at my firm co-sign?**
Yes. Add team members from **Settings → Team**. Each person's name appears on the documents they co-sign.
